sketchMechanisms: The `object` declaration creates a singleton — exactly one instance can ever exist, and it is created lazily upon first access.
Prolog's query resolution: when no facts satisfy a query, it returns `false` — not as an error, but as a logical conclusion from absence of evidence.
Go channels block on receive when empty; a goroutine waiting on a channel that no one ever sends to will wait forever, causing a fatal `deadlock` at runtime.
